scope: bounded in-process runtime chaos and recovery evidence

scenarios.claim: A slow host handler does not hide overload; fail-fast submit reports a stable runtime overload diagnostic while queued work later executes once capacity is released.
scenarios.residual_risk: This is not a durable audit-store slowdown model; it covers the in-process worker/admission surface.

scenarios.claim: Provider failure is emitted as Failed and never as Executed.
Retry after failure is explicit host work: duplicate idempotency keys are suppressed, while a new host-owned attempt can execute.
scenarios.residual_risk: First-class worker retry and hard-effect retry interleavings remain M10.2 formal-depth work.

scenarios.claim: The in-process runtime advertises no linear drain authority and reports routed contention under load without deadlock.
scenarios.residual_risk: No durable drain/fence API is added in M09.7.

scenarios.claim: A fresh in-process runtime can rejoin a partition and accept new work after the old runtime is dropped.
scenarios.residual_risk: This does not claim durable recovery of in-flight or persisted work.
